mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing activity, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year rolling window, the 20-day forward return after INDPRO increases is negative more than 50% of the time, or if the correlation between INDPRO changes and subsequent SPX returns is not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above the 2.5% annual threshold signals that the Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical tightening cycles follow high inflation
failure_condition: If over any 3-year window, periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% are followed by positive SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or if the rank IC of the inflation signal is not negative with statistical significance (p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicate the economy is overheating, which compels the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates, thereby lowering equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL increase from the previous month
failure_condition: If in months where both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL rise, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time over a 5-year rolling window, or if the interaction term in a regression is not significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2%, an increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals robust housing demand that supports economic growth and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year rolling window, periods with FEDFUNDS below 2% and rising HOUST are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the conditional correlation is not significantly positive (p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer spending, which directly improves current economic activity and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 2-year rolling window, the 5-day return after RSAFS increases is negative more than 50% of the time, or if the short-term correlation is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of at least 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period signals the onset of a recessionary labor market contraction, which depresses corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in instances where UNRATE rises 0.5% in 3 months, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time over any 10-year period, or if the signal does not significantly predict negative returns (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) rise month over month, it confirms synchronized expansion in both supply and demand sides of the economy, boosting corporate earnings and market sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If over any 5-year window, the 20-day return following simultaneous increases is negative in more than 40% of cases, or if the interaction term is not significantly positive (p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is above 6%, month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) are strongly positive for equities because they signal that the labor market recovery is accelerating, boosting consumer income and spending expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year rolling window, during periods with UNRATE > 6%, rising PAYEMS is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in less than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p>0.05).
